name: Registry Browser
description: Public content browsing for a design registry. Search, filter, and explore patterns, themes, blueprints, archetypes, and shells.

pages.notes: Marketing landing for the registry. Hero with search, featured content grid, stats.
Public user profile showing published items and activity.

page_briefs.homepage: Registry landing with prominent search bar at top, featured content grid below, and a concise registry stats section. Search is the hero, but the page should still feel editorial and curated.
page_briefs.browse: Search-first browsing page. The filter bar should stay responsive and high-signal, while cards emphasize name, description, and one concise trust summary instead of a wall of pills.
page_briefs.browse-type: Filtered registry view for a specific supported content type. Same search and filter bar layout as main browse page, pre-filtered to the selected type. Cards keep the type-specific identity but stay concise and calm.
page_briefs.detail: Content detail page. Hero card at top with item name, namespace, version, concise trust signal, and a quick-start action rail. Below, compact trust summaries and a premium artifact viewer showing the contract JSON.
page_briefs.profile: Public profile showing avatar, username, bio, published item count. Below: grid of their published content cards plus activity timeline.

shells.top-nav-main: Top navigation bar with main content area
